title: Getting Started with Codex Cryptica: A Comprehensive Guide
description: A complete step-by-step introduction to managing your tabletop worldbuilding notes, interactive spatial graphs, timelines, and AI co-authoring tools locally and privately.

title: Revising Your Lore with the Oracle: AI as Editor, Not Author
description: The Lore Oracle is not just for generating new content. You can direct it to revise existing entries — keeping authorship in your hands while it handles the rewrite.

title: AI Slop Happens When the Tool Has No Memory
description: Generic AI worldbuilding output is not a prose problem. It is a context problem — and the fix is structure, memory, and accountability to the world that already exists.

title: Six Ways to Use AI in Campaign Prep Without Losing Your Voice
description: Practical AI use cases for GMs that save time without replacing creative judgement. Each one works from your existing lore, not from a blank prompt.

title: Drafts Are Not Canon: Keeping AI Suggestions Under Control
description: AI suggestions and saved lore are not the same thing. Here is how Codex Cryptica keeps them separate so your campaign world stays yours.

title: Why Worldbuilding AI Should Know Your Lore Before It Speaks
description: Generic AI output is a context problem. AI worldbuilding tools produce bland results because they have no memory of the world they are supposed to help with.

title: A Worldbuilding Tool Should Still Work Without AI
description: If a worldbuilding tool only works when AI is switched on, it is probably not a worldbuilding tool first. Here is what that means for Codex Cryptica.

title: How Codex Cryptica Uses AI Responsibly in RPG Worldbuilding
description: The AI assistant that respects your canon, navigates your notes, and leaves the storytelling to you.

title: Introducing Context-Aware Entity Generation: Grounded Campaign Expansion
description: Branch your campaign setting organically. Discover how the new Generate Related feature pulls context from surrounding nodes and chronicles to draft connected entities with the help of Gemini.

title: No More Blank Pages: Introducing Default Entity Templates
description: Start your worldbuilding instantly. Learn how Codex Cryptica's new customizable, theme-aware default templates eliminate note-taking friction and structure your RPG campaigns.

title: Cloud Sync is Here: Back Up Your Vault to Google Drive
description: Your campaign world can now live on Google Drive — and you can share it with a co-GM in seconds. Here's how Codex Cryptica's new cloud sync works.

title: Stop Alt-Tabbing: Your Notes Are Now Playable
description: Experience a data-native VTT that turns your lore into a playable space with zero setup, instant rendering, and total privacy.

title: Why Codex Cryptica Takes a Different Approach Than Obsidian
description: Why Codex Cryptica is shaped around worldbuilding from the start, instead of asking you to assemble the workflow yourself.

title: The Front Page: Your World's Living Briefing Room
description: See your world at a glance with a front page that brings together the briefing, pinned notes, recent activity, and cover art in one place.

title: From Lore Bible to Living Graph: How the Import Feature Works
description: A deep dive into the Codex Cryptica import pipeline — from dropping a PDF onto the screen to having a fully linked, AI-extracted knowledge graph ready for your next session.

title: Your AI Co-GM: A Complete Guide to the Lore Oracle's Superpowers
description: From instant dice rolls to AI-powered story tension analysis and image generation, discover every command the Lore Oracle has to offer and how to use them to run richer sessions.

title: Supercharged Discovery: Mastering Filters and Labels in your Codex
description: Turn your pile of notes into a high-definition world. Learn how to use labels and smart filters to uncover hidden patterns in your campaign.

title: The Ultimate Guide to Mastering Codex Cryptica
description: Everything you need to know to build, manage, and synchronize your RPG campaign world using our local-first architecture.

title: The GM's Guide to Data Sovereignty: Your World, Your Files
description: Why 'Local-First' is the most important feature you didn't know you needed for your RPG campaign and how to keep your data private and portable.

title: Spatial Intelligence: How your Map, Graph, and Canvas Work Together
description: A campaign is more than just a list of names. Discover how Codex Cryptica links your Map, Graph, and Canvas to give you a 4D view of your world-building.
